title: Decorators, Cross-Cutting Behavior, and Transparent Wrappers · Effects Defense
summary: Add retries, timing, authorization checks, and telemetry without changing business function contracts. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a transparent decorator stack with order tests. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate decorator syntax into explicit rebinding and predict application order.
Separate decorator configuration, function wrapping, and call execution across closure layers.
Preserve signatures, metadata, return values, exceptions, and async behavior through wrappers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Decorator Desugaring value-and-effect pipeline for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Decorator Desugaring assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Create two validators with thresholds 25 and 30. Their captured settings must remain isolated across repeated calls, and wrappers must preserve names, signatures, returns, and exceptions.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Decorator Desugaring has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Create two validators with thresholds 25 and 30. Their captured settings must remain isolated across repeated calls, and wrappers must preserve names, signatures, returns, and exceptions.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Decorators run only when the decorated function is called.

options.text: Separate immutable values from observable effects and ownership boundaries
Specify a total transformation contract and explicit failure representation
Compose and run the smallest pipeline while retaining an inspectable trace
Repeat with changed input, verify input preservation, and move effects to the shell
